        all_items.append((f\"{os.path.basename(src)} | {name}\", amt))
all_items=sorted(all_items, key=lambda x:x[1], reverse=True)
for idx,(name,amt) in enumerate(all_items, start+1):
    ws.cell(idx,1,name)
    ws.cell(idx,2,amt)

chart=BarChart()
chart.title='High-expense Items Comparison'
chart.y_axis.title='Expense Item'
chart.x_axis.title='Amount'
chart.type='bar'
chart.style=10
chart.height=12
chart.width=24
data=Reference(ws, min_col=2, min_row=start, max_row=start+len(all_items))
cats=Reference(ws, min_col=1, min_row=start+1, max_row=start+len(all_items))
chart.add_data(data, titles_from_data=True)
chart.set_categories(cats)
ws.add_chart(chart,'G4')

for col,width in {'A':55,'B':8,'C':24,'D':14,'E':65}.items():
    ws.column_dimensions[col].width=width

notes=wb.create_sheet('Notes')
notes.append(['Notes'])
notes['A1'].font=Font(bold=True,size=14)
notes.append(['1. Data was extracted only from semfs semantic-search excerpts, per workspace instructions.'])
notes.append(['2. The dashboard/profit-statement workbooks did not expose complete itemized expense category tables in accessible excerpts; thus top-5 entries for those files are based on accessible expense figures in the excerpts.'])
notes.append(['3. The flagship store salary structure workbook was marked COMPLETE FILE, so category totals were computed directly from the full excerpt.'])

wb.save(out)
print(out, os.path.getsize(out))
